text: The surgeon uses scissors to stretch the rectus sheath, carefully opening the scissors to minimize bleeding while applying force.
The surgeon places a blunt trocar into the morcellator port, preparing for tissue morcellation during the myomectomy procedure.
The surgeon demonstrates the use of a surgeon-friendly morcellator, showing the adjustable beak and switching between safe and cut modes using a foot switch.

The surgeon switches from a grasper to a tenaculum to achieve a better grip on a hard fibroid, ensuring the instrument does not interfere with the morcellator blade. The tenaculum provides a secure hold, preventing the fibroid from slipping back into the cavity during cutting.
The surgeon uses a new morselator blade to efficiently cut through the fibroid tissue, demonstrating effective slicing action.

The surgeon demonstrates alternative methods for fibroid removal, including slicing and bisecting the fibroid using two tooth needle holders. After bisection, the fibroid can be removed through a ten mm trocar or via colpotomy from the posterior pouch.
